channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Wildfire smoke engulfs Toronto, Canada and turns its skies orange
item.description: Wildfire smoke turned skies orange over Toronto and the rest of Ontario.

item.title: ‘Epic squared’: Scaloni lauds Argentina’s back-to-back World Cup comebacks
item.description: 'There was blood in the water, and we went for it,' Argentina coach said of the relentless late attacks against England.

item.title: Iran war live: Sirens in Bahrain, Kuwait; US ‘disables’ Iranian oil tanker
item.description: Iranian media report explosions on Qeshm Island, Bandar Abbas and Chabahar, as Bahrain and Kuwait sound air raid sirens.

item.title: Tuchel defends tactics after Argentina send England packing from World Cup
item.description: England manager Tuchel swapped Reece James for Dan Burn, and Declan Rice for defender Nico O’Reilly in the 82nd minute.

item.title: Argentina players brandish political Falklands flag after England match
item.description: The banner, held up by Lisandro Martinez and Giovani Lo Celso, goes against FIFA’s Stadium Code of Conduct.

item.title: US Justice Department refuses New Mexico’s request for Epstein files
item.description: New Mexico says the withheld records are critical to its criminal investigation into alleged abuse at Epstein’s ranch.

item.title: ‘Really big news’: What to know about Trump’s primetime speech on Thursday
item.description: The address comes as the White House revives scrutiny of the 2020 election and pushes new voting restrictions.

item.title: Iran says peace deal voided, fighting ‘existential war’ after US attacks
item.description: The US announced a new wave of attacks Wednesday night, amid reported explosions in Bandar Abbas, Chabahar and Ahvaz.

item.title: Argentina stun England in 2-1 comeback win to reach 2026 World Cup final
item.description: Holders Argentina will face Spain in the final after snatching victory from England in ⁠a highly charged encounter.

item.title: xAI sues user for exploiting AI tool to sexualise minors
item.description: Lawsuit accuses Terry Harwood of misusing xAI to bypass safeguards and produce explicit deepfakes involving minors.

item.title: Authors, publishers sue Google over alleged AI copyright infringement
item.description: Hachette and Elsevier lead US legal action against Google, alleging misuse of books for Gemini AI model training.

item.title: Sheinbaum rejects US claim that Mexico’s government is linked to cartels
item.description: Sheinbaum has denounced remarks from DEA head Terry Cole as a baseless 'political statement' about Mexico.

item.title: What are the obstacles to peace between Israel and Lebanon?
item.description: Another round of talks is under way between Israel and Lebanon to try to advance the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: What happened in the first half of the England vs Argentina semifinal?
item.description: All the biggest moments, heated exchanges, fouls and stats from the first 45 minutes of the heated semifinal in Atlanta.

item.title: 10 years on from Turkiye’s failed coup attempt on Erdogan
item.description: 10 years on from Turkiye’s coup attempt to overthrow President Erdogan, the country remains divided over its legacy.

item.title: Pezeshkian vows Iran will defend ‘every inch’ of its territory
item.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian vowed Iran will defend every inch of its territory, saying attempts to tear the country apar

item.title: Russia pounds Ukraine with missile, drone strikes
item.description: Footage shows firefighters extinguishing a massive fire after Russia launched air strikes on Ukraine.

item.title: French Parliament approves landmark assisted-dying bill
item.description: Parliament adopted the text by 291 votes to 241, though it still needs approval from the Constitutional Council.

item.title: Ukraine strikes 20 Russian vessels
item.description: Ukraine released video of strikes on 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea, including 17 oil tankers.

item.title: The history of the US and Iraq’s complicated relationship
item.description: As Iraq’s prime minister visits Washington seeking deeper economic ties.

item.title: Protests in Paris, Gaza for release of Hussam Abu Safia
item.description: Protests were held in Paris and Gaza demanding the release of Palestinian doctor Hussam Abu Safia.

item.title: Fourteen-year-old boy charged over alleged plot targeting London mosques
item.description: Suspect is charged with 'preparation of terrorist acts - linked to extreme right-wing terrorism', say British police.

item.title: Argentina fans flood Atlanta ahead of England World Cup match
item.description: Thousands of Argentina fans held a banderazo in the streets of Atlanta ahead of their World Cup semi-final.

item.title: Syrian authorities arrest ex-officer accused of chemical weapons crimes
item.description: Former colonel Ahmed Habib Ali is accused of supervising the manufacture of sarin gas bombs.

item.title: European Athletics moves to curb on-screen sexualisation of female athletes
item.description: The guidelines are based on feedback from athletes who said certain camera shots can cause discomfort and distractions.
